param.description: x/y resolution of image 2.

param.name: operation
param.description: operation to be performed with the two input images.

param.name: outImg
param.description: output image as succession of rgb values.

method.name: createTexture
method.description: Creates a planar shape, that will be textured with a new, or imported texture

param.name: fileName
param.description: filename of the texure to import, or an empty string if you wish to create a new texture.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): do not interpolate adjacent color patches.
bit1 set (2): apply the texture in decal-mode.
bit2 set (4): repeat the texture along the U direction.
bit3 set (8): repeat the texture along the V direction.

param.name: planeSizes
param.description: set (1): do not interpolate adjacent color patches.

param.name: scalingUV
param.description: set (2): apply the texture in decal-mode.

param.name: xy_g
param.description: set (4): repeat the texture along the U direction.

param.name: fixedResolution
param.description: set (8): repeat the texture along the V direction.

param.name: resolution
param.description: array of 2 values: the dimensions of the planar shape that will be generated. Can be None/nil for default dimensions.

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of the created planar shape

param.name: id
param.description: new texture ID. If a same texture is already present, the old texture ID will be returned

param.name: res
param.description: the effective texture resolution

method.name: generateShapeFromPath
method.description: Generates an extrusion shape from a path

param.name: path
param.description: path, specified in row-major order, with [x y z qx qy qz qw] values for each path point. The orientation parts for each path point are ignored if the two first bits of are zero.

param.name: section
param.description: extrusion profile, with x/y pairs, e.g. [x1 y1 x2 y2 ... xn yn]. If the first and last pair overlap, the section is closed

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0-bit1: 0 = upVector is upVector , 1 = upVector is the x-axis of the path point frame, 2 = upVector is the y-axis of the path point frame, 3 = upVector is the z-axis of the path point frame
bit2 set (4): the path is closed

param.name: upVector
param.description: bit0-bit1: 0 = upVector is , 1 = upVector is the x-axis of the path point frame, 2 = upVector is the y-axis of the path point frame, 3 = upVector is the z-axis of the path point frame

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: the handle of the generated shape

method.name: generateTextShape
method.description: Generates a 3D text model.

param.name: txt
param.description: the text string

param.name: color
param.description: the text color. Optional

param.name: height
param.description: the height of the text. Optional

param.name: centered
param.description: whether the text should be centered. Optional

param.name: alphabetLocation
param.description: the path to an alphabet model. Optional

param.name: modelHandle
param.description: handle of the generated model

method.name: generateTimeOptimalTrajectory
method.description: Generates a time optimal trajectory, based on the TOPPRA library

param.name: path
param.description: path, specified in row-major order, e.g. a path containing two 3D positions would be [x1 y1 z1 x2 y2 z2]

param.name: pathLengths
param.description: lengths of a path. Each path point should have a corresponding length value (as the distance from the path's first point, along the path). See also sim.getPathLengths .

param.name: minMaxVel
param.description: minimum and maximum allowed velocity, for each dimension/axis, e.g. {xmin,xmax,ymin,ymax,zmin,zmax}

param.name: minMaxAccel
param.description: minimum and maximum allowed acceleration, for each dimension/axis, e.g. [xmin xmax ymin ymax zmin zmax]

param.name: trajPtSamples
param.description: desired number of path points/configurations

param.name: boundaryCondition
param.description: boundary condition

param.name: timeout
param.description: a timeout duration

param.name: calculationScriptHandle
param.description: optional. Can indicate a script handle returned in a previous call to this function, including -1 to indicate that the created calculation script should be returned for future reuse

param.name: outP
param.description: generated path points

param.name: times
param.description: generated times corresponding to path points

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: the handle of the calculation script for reuse in a subsequent call to this function

method.name: getClosestPosOnPath
method.description: Returns the position or distance along a path that is closest to a specified point in space

param.name: path
param.description: path, specified in row-major order, with x/y/z values for each path point

param.name: pathLengths
param.description: lengths of a path. Each path point should have a corresponding length value (as the distance from the path's first point, along the path). See also sim.getPathLengths .

param.name: absPt
param.description: point in 3D space

param.name: posAlongPath
param.description: position/distance along the path

method.name: getConfigDistance
method.description: Returns the distance between two configurations points

param.name: configA
param.description: the first configuration, e.g. in case of a 3D pose (position+quaternion), would be [x1 y1 z1 qx1 qy1 qz1 qw1]

param.name: configB
param.description: the second configuration

param.name: metric
param.description: an optional array specifying the metric to use to compute distances. e.g. if the specified configurations were 3D positions, the distance between two configurations would be calculated as SQRT( mx * (x2 - x1)^2 + my * (y2 - y1)^2 + mz * (z2 - z1)^2 ), where [mx my mz] would be the metric.

param.name: types
param.description: an optional array specifying the type of each configuration value/dimension: 0=cartesian value, 1=2pi-cyclic value, 2=quaternion value. e.g. a configuration representing 3D poses should use a types argument [0 0 0 2 2 2 2], a configuration representing revolute and cyclic joints should use a types argument [1 1 1 ...]

param.name: distance
param.description: the distance between the two configurations

method.name: getContactInfo
method.description: Retrieves contact point information of a dynamic simulation pass.

param.name: dynamicPass
param.description: a specific dynamic sub-step index or sim.handle_all. By default a call to simHandleDynamics executes the dynamics engine x times, with x times smaller time steps (where x is a parameter that can be adjusted). At each of those sub-steps, contacts are created and destroyed. With the dynamicPass argument you can select which sub-step you wish to retrieve contacts from (zero-based index), or sim.handle_all to retrieve the contacts of all sub-steps. See also the dynamicsStepSize property.

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of a specific object you wish to retrieve contacts from, or sim.handle_all to retrieve all contacts in the scene.

param.name: index
param.description: zero-based index of the contact to retrieve

param.name: coll
param.description: handles of the two objects contacting. The handles might also refer to particle objects that are not treated as regular scene objects

param.name: point
param.description: coordinates of the contact

param.name: rForce
param.description: vector that represents the force generated by the contact

param.name: n
param.description: normal vector at the contact point

method.name: getGenesisEvents
method.description: Retrieves all events that allow to reconstruct a scene's (mostly) visual content remotely

param.name: events
param.description: the genesis events as a cbor-coded buffer

method.name: getJointDependency
method.description: Retrieves joint dependency information, when the joint is in dependent mode

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the slave joint

param.name: masterJointHandle
param.description: handle of the master joint, or -1 if the joint is not dependent

param.name: offset
param.description: offset in equation slave = offset + master * multCoeff

param.name: multCoeff
param.description: coeff in equation slave = offset + master * multCoeff

method.name: getJointForce
method.description: Retrieves the force or torque applied to a joint along/about its active axis. This function retrieves meaningful information only if the joint is prismatic or revolute, and is dynamically enabled. With the Bullet, MuJoCo and Newton engine, this function returns the force or torque applied to the joint motor (torques from joint limits are not taken into account). With the ODE and Vortex engine, this function returns the total force or torque applied to a joint along/about its z-axis

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint. Can be combined with sim.handleflag_rawvalue (simply add sim.handleflag_rawvalue to jointHandle), if you wish to access the raw values generated by each individual dynamic simulation step (by default, there are 10 dynamic simulation steps for each simulation step). Raw values can only be accessed from inside a callback function triggered by the physics engine.

param.name: forceOrTorque
param.description: force or the torque applied to the joint along/about its z-axis.

method.name: getJointInterval
method.description: Retrieves the interval parameters of a joint

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint

param.name: cyclic
param.description: indicates whether the joint is cyclic (the joint varies between -pi and +pi in a cyclic manner).

param.name: interval
param.description: interval of the joint. interval[1] is the joint minimum allowed value, interval[2] is the joint range (the maximum allowed value is interval[1]+interval[2]). When the joint is "cyclic", then the interval parameters don't have any meaning.

method.name: getJointMode
method.description: Retrieves the operation mode of a joint

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: jointMode
param.description: the joint mode

method.name: getJointPosition
method.description: Retrieves the linear/angular displacement/angle of a joint. This function cannot be used with spherical joints (use sim.getObjectChildPose instead)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint

param.name: pos
param.description: linear/angular displacement/angle of the joint

method.name: getJointTargetForce
method.description: Retrieves the force or torque that a joint can exert

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint

param.name: forceOrTorque
param.description: the maximum force or torque the joint can apply. The sign indicates the desired movement direction

method.name: getJointTargetPosition
method.description: Retrieves the target linear/angular displacement/angle of a joint

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: targetPosition
param.description: target position of the joint

method.name: getJointTargetVelocity
method.description: Retrieves the target linear/angular velocity of a non-spherical joint

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: targetVelocity
param.description: target velocity of the joint.

method.name: getJointType
method.description: Retrieves the type of a joint

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint

param.name: jointType
param.description:
type of the joint:
sim.joint_revolute
sim.joint_prismatic
sim.joint_spherical

method.name: getJointVelocity
method.description: Retrieves the linear or angular velocity of a joint. The velocity is a measured velocity (i.e. from one simulation step to the next), and is available for all joints in the scene

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of a joint

param.name: velocity
param.description: the velocity

method.name: getLastInfo
method.description: Retrieves and clears the information string generated by last API call

param.name: info
param.description: the information string

method.name: getLinkDummy
method.description: Retrieves the object handle of the dummy linked to this one

param.name: dummyHandle
param.description: handle of the dummy whose linked dummy has to be retrieved

param.name: linkedDummyHandle
param.description: handle of the dummy linked to the specified dummy object, or -1 if the dummy is not linked

method.name: getNavigationMode
method.description: Retrieves the navigation and selection mode for the mouse

method.name: getObjectChildPose
method.description: Retrieves the intrinsic or internal transformation of an object. For a joint, this is the transformation caused by the joint movement, mainly. For joints and force sensors, this will also include a possible error transformation caused by the physics engine (a physics engine can cause joints and force sensors to come apart, when constraints can't be perfectly resolved)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint

param.name: pose
param.description: the pose

method.name: getObjectColor
method.description: Retrieves the color of a scene object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: index
param.description: zero-based index of the color, if the object has several colors

param.name: rgbData
param.description: red, green and blue components of the color (3 values)

method.name: getObjectType
method.description: Retrieves the type of an object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: objectType

method.name: getObjectUid
method.description: Retrieves the unique identifier of an object: throughout a CoppeliaSim session, there won't be two identical unique identifiers. Unique identifiers are however not persistent (i.e. are not saved with the object)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: object handle

param.name: uid
param.description: the unique identifier

method.name: getObjectVelocity
method.description: Retrieves the linear and/or angular velocity of an object, in absolute coordinates. The velocity is a measured velocity (i.e. from one simulation step to the next), and is available for all objects in the scene

param.name: objectHandle
param.description:
handle of a scene object . It is highly recommended to add
sim.handleflag_axis
to the object handle, in order to obtain the
regular
angular velocity for the object, i.e. the rotation axis and the rotation velocity that is represented by the axis' length. If
sim.handleflag_axis
is omitted, then a derivative of Euler angles expressed relative to previous' simulation frame will be returned for the angular velocity

param.name: linearVelocity
param.description: array of 3 values that represent the linear velocity

param.name: angularVelocity
param.description: array of 3 values that represent the angular velocity

method.name: getOctreeVoxels
method.description: Retrieves voxel positions from an OC tree

param.name: octreeHandle
param.description: handle of the OC tree

param.name: voxels
param.description: the voxel [x y z] positions, relative to the OC tree reference frame

method.name: getPage
method.description: Retrieves the current page index (view)

param.name: pageIndex
param.description: page index

method.name: getPathInterpolatedConfig
method.description: Returns an interpolated configuration from a path

param.name: path
param.description: the path, specified in row-major order, e.g. a path containing two 3D poses (position+quaternion) would be [x1 y1 z1 qx1 qy1 qz1 qw1 x2 y2 z2 qx2 qy2 qz2 qw2]

param.name: pathLengths
param.description: the lengths of a path. Each path point should have a corresponding length value (as the distance from the path's first point, along the path). See also sim.getPathLengths .

param.name: t
param.description: the distance from the beginning of the path, where the interpolation point should be picked from

param.name: method
param.description: an optional map specifying the type of interpolation (linear or quadraticBezier), whether the path should be considered as open, even if the first and last path points overlap, and the bezier strength (0.05-1.0)

param.name: types
param.description: an optional array specifying the type of each configuration value/dimension: 0=cartesian value, 1=2pi-cyclic value, 2=quaternion value. e.g. a configuration representing 3D poses should use a types argument [0 0 0 2 2 2 2], a configuration representing revolute and cyclic joints should use a types argument [1 1 1 ...]

param.name: config
param.description: the interpolated path configuration

method.name: getPathLengths
method.description: Returns the lengths of a path in 1, 2 or 3D Cartesian space, even if more coordinates are provided. Each path point will have a corresponding length value (taken as the distance from the path's first point, along the path)

param.name: path
param.description: the path, specified in row-major order, e.g. a path containing two 3D poses (position+quaternion) would be [x1 y1 z1 qx1 qy1 qz1 qw1 x2 y2 z2 qx2 qy2 qz2 qw2]

param.name: dof
param.description: the size or dimension of path points, e.g. in case of a 3D pose, this would be 7 (however only the first 3 dimensions would be taken into account)

param.name: distCallback
param.description: an optional function that takes as input two configurations, and returns the calculated distance between them. See also sim.getConfigDistance

param.name: pathLengths
param.description: the lengths corresponding to each path point

param.name: totalLength
param.description: the total length of the path

method.name: getPluginInfo
method.description: Returns information about a loaded plugin

param.name: pluginName
param.description: name of the plugin

param.name: infoType
param.description: type of information to retrieve

param.name: info
param.description: the retrieved information

method.name: getPointCloudOptions
method.description: Gets various properties of a point cloud

param.name: pcHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: maxVoxelS
param.description: maximum size of the OC tree voxels containing points

param.name: maxPtCntPerVoxel
param.description: maximum number of points allowed in a same OC tree voxel

param.name: opt
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): points have random colors
bit1 set (2): show OC tree structure
bit2 set (4): reserved. keep unset
bit3 set (8): do not use an OC tree structure. When enabled, point cloud operations are limited, and point clouds will not be collidable , measurable or detectable anymore, but adding points will be much faster
bit4 set (16): color is emissive

param.name: ps
param.description: bit0 set (1): points have random colors

method.name: getPointCloudPoints
method.description: Retrieves point positions from a point cloud

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: points
param.description: the point [x y z] positions, relative to the point cloud reference frame

method.name: getScript
method.description: Retrieves the handle of a script. For script objects, use sim.getObject instead

param.name: scriptType
param.description: type of the script , or sim.handle_self

param.name: scriptName
param.description: name of the add-on, in case of an add-on

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: handle of the script

method.name: getShapeBB
method.description: Returns the size and relative pose of a shape's bounding box

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: size
param.description: size array [Sx Sy Sz]

param.name: pose
param.description: pose array: [x y z qx qy qz qw]. The pose is relative to the shape's pose

method.name: getShapeColor
method.description: Retrieves the color of a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: colorName
result
param.description: 0 if the color name was not found in the shape, 1 otherwise

param.name: rgbData
param.description: red, green and blue components of the color (3 values), or the transparency value (1 value)

method.name: getShapeGeomInfo
method.description: Retrieves geometric information related to a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: result
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): shape is a compound shape
bit1 set (2): shape is a primitive
bit2 set (4): shape is convex

param.name: pureType
param.description: bit0 set (1): shape is a compound shape

param.name: dimensions
param.description: bit1 set (2): shape is a primitive

method.name: getShapeMass
method.description: Retrieves the mass of a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape object

param.name: mass
param.description: mass of the shape

method.name: getShapeMesh
method.description: Retrieves a shape's mesh information

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape. See sim.exportMesh for a usage example.

param.name: vertices
param.description: array of vertices

param.name: indices
param.description: array of indices

param.name: normals
param.description: array of normals

method.name: getShapeTextureId
method.description: Retrieves the texture ID of a texture that is applied to a specific shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape.

param.name: textureId
param.description: texture ID, or -1 if texture does not exist

method.name: getShapeViz
method.description: Retrieves a shape's visual information.

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape.

param.name: index
param.description: 0-based index of the shape element to retrieve (compound shapes contain more than one shape element)

param.name: data
param.description: array containing the vertices, relative to the shape's reference frame

method.name: getSimulationState
method.description: Retrieves current simulation state

param.name: simulationState
param.description: current state of the simulation

method.name: getSimulationStopping
method.description: Convenience function that returns true when the simulation is about to stop or stopped.

param.name: stopping
param.description: if true, the simulation is about to stop, or stopped.

method.name: getSimulationTime
method.description: Retrieves the current simulation time

param.name: simulationTime
param.description: simulation time

method.name: getSimulationTimeStep
method.description: Retrieves the simulation time step (the simulation time (i.e. not real-time) that passes at each main script simulation pass). This value might not be constant for a given simulation.

param.name: timeStep
param.description: simulation time step

method.name: getSimulatorMessage
method.description: Retrieves and removes the next message in the C/C++ or Lua message queues. Use this in a while-loop until all messages have been extracted. While the C/C++ interface has one single message queue, each Lua script has its own message queue. The C/C++ version of this function should only be called from the CoppeliaSim client application. A given message queue cannot hold more than 64 messages, unread messages will be discarded.

param.name: auxiliaryData
param.description: array of numbers that describe the returned message in more details

param.name: auxiliaryData2
param.description: array of numbers that describe the returned message in more details

method.name: getSystemTime
method.description: Retrieves the system time.

param.name: systemTime
param.description: the system time

method.name: getTextureId
method.description: Retrieves the texture ID of a specific texture

param.name: textureName
param.description: name of the texture ID to be retrieved

param.name: textureId
param.description: the texture ID, or -1 if the texture does not exist

param.name: resolution
param.description: an array of 2 values representing the resolution of the texture

method.name: getUserVariables
method.description: Lua only. Returns all variables, except those set by CoppeliaSim.

param.name: variables
param.description: a map-like table with all global user variables

method.name: getVelocity
method.description: Retrieves the linear and/or angular velocity of the center of mass of a non-static shape. Data is provided by the selected physics engine

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of a dynamically enabled shape

param.name: linearVelocity
param.description: array of 3 values that represent the linear velocity in absolute coordinates

param.name: angularVelocity
param.description: array of 3 values that represent the angular velocity in absolute coordinates. The length of the vector represents the rotation speed, and the vector direction represents the rotation axis passing through the center of mass of the shape

method.name: handleDynamics
method.description: Handles the dynamics functionality in a scene

param.name: deltaTime
param.description: simulation time step, i.e. sim.getSimulationTimeStep ()

param.name: result
param.description: 0 if the sepcified physics engine could not be found, otherwise the number of calculation steps performed by the physics engine.

method.name: handleExtCalls
method.description: Handles the message pump for threaded scripts

method.name: handleSensingStart
method.description:
Handles various functionality (e.g. camera tracking during simulation, object velocity calculation, etc.). Should only be called from the main script , as the first instruction in the
sensing
section

method.name: handleSimulationStart
method.description:
Initializes various functionality (e.g. camera tracking during simulation, object velocity calculation, etc.). Should only be called from the main script , as the first instruction in the
initialization
section

method.name: importMesh
method.description: Imports a mesh from a file

param.name: fileformat
param.description: set to 0. Fileformat is automatically detected

param.name: pathAndFilename
param.description: the location of the file to import.

param.name: options
param.description: bit-coded: bit0 set (1): keep identical vertices, bit7 set (128): ignore up-vector coded in fileformat

param.name: identicalVerticeTolerance
param.description: has no effect. set to zero

param.name: scalingFactor
param.description: the scaling factor to apply to the imported vertices

param.name: vertices
param.description: an array of vertice arrays. The import operation may generate several meshes depending on the fileformat

param.name: indices
param.description: an array of index arrays. The import operation may generate several meshes depending on the fileformat

more.code:
--lua
local vertices, indices, reserved, names = sim.importMesh(1, "d:\\example.dxf", 0, 0.0001, 1) for i = 1, #vertices, 1 do h = sim.createShape(2, 20 * math.pi / 180, vertices[i], indices[i]) sim.setShapeColor(h, "", sim.colorcomponent_ambient, {0.5, 0.5, 0.5}) sim.setObjectAlias(h, names[i]) end

method.name: importShape
method.description: Imports a shape from a file (first imports meshes, then groups/merges them into a shape)

param.name: fileformat
param.description: set to 0. Fileformat is automatically detected

param.name: pathAndFilename
param.description: the location of the file to import.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): keep identical vertices
bit1 set (2): reserced. keep at 0.
bit2 set (4): reserved. keep at 0.
bit3 set (8): do not preserve colors
bit4 set (16): tries to preserve textures.
bit5 set (32): realigns the shape's bounding box with the world.
bit7 set (128): ignore up-vector coded in fileformat.

param.name: identicalVerticeTolerance
param.description: bit0 set (1): keep identical vertices

param.name: scalingFactor
param.description: bit1 set (2): reserced. keep at 0.

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: the handle of the imported shape

method.name: isDynamicallyEnabled
method.description: Checks whether a scene object is dynamically enabled, i.e. is being handled and simulated by the physics engine. Note that until the physics engine has parsed the scene in the first simulation step (i.e. the first time sim.handleDynamics is called), no object will be dynamically enabled

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the scene object

param.name: dynamicallyEnabled
param.description: whether the object is dynamically enabled

method.name: isHandle
method.description: Checks whether a general object handle is still valid. When a general object is destroyed (e.g. programmatically or via the user interface), then its related handle is not valid anymore and will trigger an error when used. Use this function to avoid triggering an error

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: result
param.description: whether the handle is valid

method.name: moduleEntry
method.description: Creates, modifies or destroys module menu entries. Those are user selectable items located in [Menu bar > Modules]. When selected, the corresponding script will have its sysCall_moduleEntry callback function triggered, or sim_message_eventcallback_moduleentry triggered

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of a module menu entry, or -1 to create one.

param.name: label
param.description:
label of the module entry. If handle is -1, then a path can be specified, in order to build sub-menus, e.g. "Menu
Submenu1
Submenu2
Label". Can be nullptr if handle is not -1.

param.name: state
param.description:
Bit-coded (is ignored if set to -1, -2 destroys an existing item):
bit0 is set (1): the item is enabled
bit1 is set (2): the item is checked
bit2 is set (4): the item is checkable

param.name: handle
param.description: the handle of the module menu entry

method.name: moveToConfig
method.description: Executes a n-DoF motion using the Ruckig online trajectory generator . This function can only be called from scripts running in a thread, since this is a blocking operation

param.name: params
param.description:
parameters of the function:
pos : mandatory if joints is not specified. Specifies the current configuration as a vector with one value per DoF
joints : mandatory if pos is not specified. Specifies the joint handles as a vector with one handle per DoF
targetPos : specifies the target configuration as a vector with one value per DoF
flags : optional. Ruckig flags , -1 for default flags.
vel : optional. Specifies current velocity as a vector with one value per DoF. Defaults to [0, 0, ...] if omitted
accel : optional. Specifies current acceleration as a vector with one value per DoF. Defaults to [0, 0, ...] if omitted
maxVel : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed velocity as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to [inf, inf, ...] if omitted
minVel : optional. Specifies the minimum allowed velocity as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to -maxVel if omitted
maxAccel : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed acceleration as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to [inf, inf, ...] if omitted
minAccel : optional. Specifies the minimum allowed acceleration as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to -maxAccel if omitted
maxJerk : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed jerk as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to [inf, inf, ...] if omitted
targetVel : optional. Specifies the target velocity as a vector with one value per DoF. Defaults to [0, 0, ...] if omitted
callback : optional. Callback function called for each motion step. The argument provided to the callback function is the current motion state
auxData : optional. Data to be forwarded to the callback function
cyclicJoints : optional. Indicates which joint is cyclic (for cyclic joints, the movement always goes in the direction that represents the shortest distance to the goal). Defaults to [false, false, ...] is omitted
timeStep : optional. The desired time step size. A value of 0 indicates that the current simulation time step is used. Defaults to 0 if omitted

param.name: data
param.description: current motion state

method.name: sim.moveToConfig_init
method.description: Initiates a n-DoF motion using the Ruckig online trajectory generator

param.name: params
param.description:
parameters of the function:
pos : mandatory if joints is not specified. Specifies the current configuration as a vector with one value per DoF
joints : mandatory if pos is not specified. Specifies the joint handles as a vector with one handle per DoF
targetPos : specifies the target configuration as a vector with one value per DoF
flags : optional. Ruckig flags , -1 for default flags.
vel : optional. Specifies current velocity as a vector with one value per DoF. Defaults to [0, 0, ...] if omitted
accel : optional. Specifies current acceleration as a vector with one value per DoF. Defaults to [0, 0, ...] if omitted
maxVel : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed velocity as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to [inf, inf, ...] if omitted
minVel : optional. Specifies the minimum allowed velocity as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to -maxVel if omitted
maxAccel : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed acceleration as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to [inf, inf, ...] if omitted
minAccel : optional. Specifies the minimum allowed acceleration as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to -maxAccel if omitted
maxJerk : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed jerk as a vector with one value per DoF, or as a scalar (scaled to a DoF-vector). Defaults to [inf, inf, ...] if omitted
targetVel : optional. Specifies the target velocity as a vector with one value per DoF. Defaults to [0, 0, ...] if omitted
callback : optional. Callback function called for each motion step. The argument provided to the callback function is the current motion state
auxData : optional. Data to be forwarded to the callback function
cyclicJoints : optional. Indicates which joint is cyclic (for cyclic joints, the movement always goes in the direction that represents the shortest distance to the goal). Defaults to [false, false, ...] is omitted
timeStep : optional. The desired time step size. A value of 0 indicates that the current simulation time step is used. Defaults to 0 if omitted

param.name: motionObject
param.description: a motion object

method.name: sim.moveToConfig_step
method.description: Steps a motion object

param.name: motionObject
param.description: a motion object

param.name: res
param.description:
step result:
negative : error
0 : step successful, motion is still ongoing
1 : step successful, motion ended
2 : motion aborted

param.name: data
param.description: error

method.name: sim.moveToConfig_cleanup
method.description: Destroys a motion object

param.name: motionObject
param.description: handle of the motion object

method.name: moveToPose
method.description: Executes an object motion using the Ruckig online trajectory generator , by performing interpolations between two poses. The function can operate by handling 4 motion variables (x,y,z and angle between the two poses), or a single movement variable (t, which requires a metric to be specified for distance calculation between the two poses). This function can only be called from a script running in a thread, since this is a blocking operation

param.name: params
param.description:
parameters of the function:
pose : mandatory if neither object nor ik are specified. Specifies current pose
object : mandatory if neither pose nor ik are specified. Specifies the handle of the object to move
ik : mandatory if neither pose nor object are specified. Contains IK-relevant informations, if a kinematic chain should move via IK to a target:
tip : specifies the handle of the tip object (on the end-effector)
target : specifies the handle of the target object (the object to reach)
base : optional. Specifies the handle of the base object (the base of the kinematic chain). Defaults to -1
joints : optional. Specifies the handles of the joints involved in IK calculations. Defaults to all joints within tip and base
method : optional. Specifies the resolution method. Defaults to simIK.method_damped_least_squares
damping : optional. Specifies the resolution damping. Defaults to 0.02
iterations : optional. Specifies the max. number of iterations. Defaults to 20
constraints : optional. Specifies the constraints. Defaults to simIK.constraint_pose
precision : optional. Specifies the desired precision. Defaults to [0.001, 0.5 * math.pi / 180.0]
allowError : optional. Specifies whether a resolution with precision values over what is allowed will be applied anyway. Defaults to params.ik.allowError
breakFlags : optional. Specified the reasons-flags required for simIK.handleGroup to fail Defaults to 0
targetPose : specifies the target pose
targetVel : optional. Specifies the target velocity. Defaults to [0, ...] if omitted
flags : optional. Ruckig flags , -1 for default flags.
metric : optional. A metric to compute a pose-pose distance. If omitted, the motion calculation happens in the 4-dimensional space (dim = 4: x, y, z, angle), otherwise it happens in the 1-dimensional space (dim = 1: t)
maxVel : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed velocity as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to [inf, ...] if omitted
minVel : optional. Specifies the minimum allowed velocity as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to -maxVel if omitted
maxAccel : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed acceleration as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to [inf, ...] if omitted
minAccel : optional. Specifies the minimum allowed acceleration as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to -maxAccel if omitted
maxJerk : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed jerk as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to [inf, ...] if omitted
callback : optional. Callback function called for each motion step. The argument provided to the callback function is the current motion state
auxData : optional. Data to be forwarded to the callback function
timeStep : optional. The desired time step size. A value of 0 indicates that the current simulation time step is used. Defaults to 0 if omitted

param.name: data
param.description: current motion state

method.name: sim.moveToPose_init
method.description: Initiates an object motion using the Ruckig online trajectory generator , by performing interpolations between two poses. The function can operate by handling 4 motion variables (x,y,z and angle between the two poses), or a single movement variable (t, which requires a metric to be specified for distance calculation between the two poses)

param.name: params
param.description:
parameters of the function:
pose : mandatory if neither object nor ik are specified. Specifies current pose
object : mandatory if neither pose nor ik are specified. Specifies the handle of the object to move
ik : mandatory if neither pose nor object are specified. Contains IK-relevant informations, if a kinematic chain should move via IK to a target:
tip : specifies the handle of the tip object (on the end-effector)
target : specifies the handle of the target object (the object to reach)
base : optional. Specifies the handle of the base object (the base of the kinematic chain). Defaults to -1
joints : optional. Specifies the handles of the joints involved in IK calculations. Defaults to all joints within tip and base
method : optional. Specifies the resolution method. Defaults to simIK.method_damped_least_squares
damping : optional. Specifies the resolution damping. Defaults to 0.02
iterations : optional. Specifies the max. number of iterations. Defaults to 20
constraints : optional. Specifies the constraints. Defaults to simIK.constraint_pose
precision : optional. Specifies the desired precision. Defaults to [0.001, 0.5 * math.pi / 180.0]
allowError : optional. Specifies whether a resolution with precision values over what is allowed will be applied anyway. Defaults to params.ik.allowError
breakFlags : optional. Specified the reasons-flags required for simIK.handleGroup to fail Defaults to 0
targetPose : specifies the target pose
targetVel : optional. Specifies the target velocity. Defaults to [0, ...] if omitted
flags : optional. Ruckig flags , -1 for default flags.
metric : optional. A metric to compute a pose-pose distance. If omitted, the motion calculation happens in the 4-dimensional space (dim = 4: x, y, z, angle), otherwise it happens in the 1-dimensional space (dim = 1: t)
maxVel : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed velocity as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to [inf, ...] if omitted
minVel : optional. Specifies the minimum allowed velocity as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to -maxVel if omitted
maxAccel : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed acceleration as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to [inf, ...] if omitted
minAccel : optional. Specifies the minimum allowed acceleration as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to -maxAccel if omitted
maxJerk : optional. Specifies the maximum allowed jerk as a vector with one value per dim, or as a scalar (scaled to a dim-vector). Defaults to [inf, ...] if omitted
callback : optional. Callback function called for each motion step. The argument provided to the callback function is the current motion state
auxData : optional. Data to be forwarded to the callback function
timeStep : optional. The desired time step size. A value of 0 indicates that the current simulation time step is used. Defaults to 0 if omitted

param.name: motionObject
param.description: a motion object

method.name: readTexture
method.description: Retrieves the RGB data (or a portion of it) related to a specific texture

param.name: textureId
param.description: ID of the texture. See also simGetTextureId .

param.name: options
param.description: reserved for future functionality. Set to zero.

param.name: posX
param.description: x/y position of the texture portion to retrieve. Set to [0 0] to retrieve the full texture

param.name: posY
param.description: x/y size of the texture portion to retrieve. Set to [0 0] to retrieve the full texture

param.name: textureData
param.description: texture data

method.name: refreshDialogs
method.description: Refreshes CoppeliaSim's internal dialogs. Calling sim.refreshDialogs will not trigger a sim.message_eventcallback_refreshdialogs message

param.name: refreshDegree
param.description: refresh degree (0=light, 1=medium, 2=full)

method.name: removeParticleObject
method.description: Removes a previously added particle object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of a previously added particle object. sim.handle_all removes all particle objects from the scene

method.name: resamplePath
method.description: Returns a resampled path

param.name: path
param.description: path, specified in row-major order, e.g. a path containing two 3D poses (position+quaternion) would be [x1 y1 z1 qx1 qy1 qz1 qw1 x2 y2 z2 qx2 qy2 qz2 qw2]

param.name: pathLengths
param.description: lengths of a path. Each path point should have a corresponding length value (as the distance from the path's first point, along the path). See also sim.getPathLengths .

param.name: finalConfigCnt
param.description: number of points or configurations the resampled path should contain

param.name: method
param.description: optional map specifying the type of interpolation (linear or quadraticBezier), and whether the path should be considered as open, even if the first and last path points overlap, and the bezier strength (0.05-1.0)

param.name: types
param.description: optional array specifying the type of each configuration value/dimension: 0=cartesian value, 1=2pi-cyclic value, 2=quaternion value. e.g. a configuration representing 3D poses should use a types argument [0 0 0 2 2 2 2], a configuration representing revolute and cyclic joints should use a types argument [1 1 1 ...]

param.name: outputPath
param.description: the resampled path

method.name: ruckigPos
method.description: Executes a call to the Ruckig online trajectory generator . The Ruckig online trajectory generator provides instantaneous trajectory generation capabilities for motion control systems. This function prepares a position-based trajectory generation object, that can then be calculated with sim.ruckigStep . When this object is not needed anymore, remove it with sim.ruckigRemove

param.name: dofs
param.description: number of degrees of freedom (n).

param.name: baseCycleTime
param.description: smallest expected cycle time. The cycle time should always be a multiple of baseCycleTime. Use a value of 0.0001 (0.1ms).

param.name: flags
param.description: Ruckig flags . -1 for default flags.

param.name: currentPosVelAccel
param.description: current position, velocity and acceleration: [pos_1 ... pos_n vel_1 ... vel_n accel_1 ... accel_n] (one value for each DoF)

param.name: maxVelAccelJerk
param.description: maximum allowed velocity, acceleration and jerk: [maxV_1 ... maxV_n maxA_1 ... maxA_n maxJ_1 ... maxJ_n]. If sim.ruckig_minvel is specified in flags, then maxVelAccelJerk should contain following additional values: [minV_1 ... minV_n]. If sim.ruckig_minaccel is specified in flags, then maxVelAccelJerk should contain following additional values: [minA_1 ... minA_n]

param.name: selection
param.description: selection vector (one value for each DoF). For a default behaviour, fill the vector with non-zero values.

param.name: targetPosVel
param.description: target position and velocity: [tPos_1 ... tPos_n tVel_1 ... tVel_n]

param.name: handle
param.description: the handle to the created object

method.name: ruckigRemove
method.description: Removes an object previously created via sim.ruckigPos or sim.ruckigVel .

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of the object created via sim.ruckigPos or sim.ruckigVel .

method.name: ruckigStep
method.description: Executes a call to the Ruckig online trajectory generator . The Ruckig online trajectory generator provides instantaneous trajectory generation capabilities for motion control systems. This function steps forward a trajectory generation algorithm previously prepared via sim.ruckigPos or sim.ruckigVel

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of the object created via sim.ruckigPos or sim.ruckigVel .

param.name: cycleTime
param.description: cycle time or simulation step. Should always be a multiple of the base cycle time

param.name: result
param.description:
return value of the update function in the motion library:
1: Result::Finished (final state reached)
0: Result::Working (final state not yet reached)
-100: Result::ErrorInvalidInput
-101: Result::ErrorTrajectoryDuration
-110: Result::ErrorExecutionTimeCalculation
-111: Result::ErrorSynchronizationCalculation

param.name: newPosVelAccel
param.description: 1: Result::Finished (final state reached)

param.name: synchronizationTime
param.description: 0: Result::Working (final state not yet reached)

method.name: ruckigVel
method.description: Executes a call to the Ruckig online trajectory generator . The Ruckig online trajectory generator provides instantaneous trajectory generation capabilities for motion control systems. This function prepares a velocity-based trajectory generation object, that can then be calculated with sim.ruckigStep . When this object is not needed anymore, remove it with sim.ruckigRemove

param.name: dofs
param.description: number of degrees of freedom (n).

param.name: baseCycleTime
param.description: smallest expected cycle time. The cycle time should always be a multiple of baseCycleTime. Use a value of 0.0001 (0.1ms).

param.name: flags
param.description: Ruckig flags . -1 for default flags.

param.name: currentPosVelAccel
param.description: current position, velocity and acceleration: [pos_1 ... pos_n vel_1 ... vel_n accel_1 ... accel_n] (one value for each DoF)

param.name: maxAccelJerk
param.description: maximum allowed acceleration and jerk: [maxA_1 ... maxA_n maxJ_1 ... maxJ_n]. If sim.ruckig_minaccel is specified in flags, then maxAccelJerk should contain following additional values: [minA_1 ... minA_n]

param.name: selection
param.description: selection vector (one value for each DoF). For a default behaviour, fill the vector with non-zero values.

param.name: targetVel
param.description: target velocity (one value for each DoF)

param.name: handle
param.description: the handle of the created object

method.name: setAutoYieldDelay
method.description: Allows specifying a thread interruption or yield delay, that will be automatically enforced by the system (preemptive threading). By default this value is 2 ms

param.name: dt
param.description: desired execution time, before an interruption or yield occurs

method.name: setJointDependency
method.description: Sets a joint dependent of another joint. The dependent joint should first be set into dependent mode via sim.setJointMode

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint to become slave

param.name: masterJointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint to become master, or -1 to free the slave joint from dependency

param.name: offset
param.description: offset in equation slave = offset + master * multCoeff

param.name: multCoeff
param.description: coeff in equation slave = offset + master * multCoeff

method.name: setNavigationMode
method.description: Sets the navigation and selection mode for the mouse

method.name: setObjectChildPose
method.description: Can be used to set a spherical joint's rotational transformation (the translational part is ignored)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: pose
param.description: the pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

method.name: setObjectColor
method.description: Sets the color of a scene object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: index
param.description: zero-based index of the color, if the object has several colors

param.name: rgbData
param.description: red, green and blue components of the color (3 values)

method.name: setPage
method.description: Switches between pages (main scene views)

param.name: index
param.description: index of the page. Valid values are 0-7

method.name: setPluginInfo
method.description: Attaches additional information to a loaded plugin

param.name: pluginName
param.description: name of the plugin

param.name: infoType
param.description: the type of information to set

param.name: info
param.description: string or integer information value

method.name: setPointCloudOptions
method.description: Sets various properties of a point cloud

param.name: pcHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: maxVoxelSize
param.description: maximum size of the OC tree voxels containing points

param.name: maxPtCntPerVoxel
param.description: maximum number of points allowed in a same OC tree voxel

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): points have random colors
bit1 set (2): show OC tree structure
bit2 set (4): reserved. keep unset
bit3 set (8): do not use an OC tree structure. When enabled, point cloud operations are limited, and point clouds will not be collidable , measurable or detectable anymore, but adding points will be much faster
bit4 set (16): color is emissive

param.name: pointSize
param.description: bit0 set (1): points have random colors

method.name: setShapeBB
method.description: Sets the size of a shape's bounding box, effectively scaling the shape. Non-isometric scaling is not always possible

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: size
param.description: size of the bounding box

method.name: setShapeColor
method.description: Sets the color of a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: colorName
rgbData
param.description: red, green and blue components of the color (3 values), or the transparency value (1 value)

method.name: setShapeMass
method.description: Applies a new mass value to a shape. If simulation is running, the shape is dynamically reset (similar to calling sim.resetDynamicObject right after)

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape object

param.name: mass
param.description: new mass of the shape

method.name: textEditorClose

param.description: handle of the text editor window

param.description: text of the editor

param.name: pos
param.description: size of the editor

method.description: Retieves information from a text edition window previously opened with sim.textEditorOpen

param.description: text of the editor, or None/nil if the given handle is not associated with any text editor window (or the window was already closed)

param.name: pos
param.description: size of the editor

param.name: visible
param.description: visible state the editor

method.name: textEditorOpen
method.description: Opens a text edition window

param.name: initText
param.description: initial text to be displayed.

param.name: properties
param.description: XML description of the text editor's properties. Can be an empty string for default properties

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of the text editor

more.code:
<editor title="Window title" editable="true" searchable="true" tab-width="4" toolbar="false" statusbar="false" resizable="true" modal="false" on-close="callbackFunction" closeable="true" position="100 100" size="800 600" placement="absolute" font="Courier" font-size="14" activate="false" editable="true" line-numbers="true" max-lines="0" lang="lua" wrap-word="false" text-col="50 50 50" background-col="190 190 190" selectionColor="128 128 255" comment-col="0 140 0" number-col="220 0 220" string-col="255 255 0" character-col="255 255 0" operator-col="0 0 0" identifier-col="64 64 64" preprocessor-col="0 128 128" keyword1-col="152 0 0" keyword2-col="220 80 20" keyword3-col="0 0 255" keyword4-col="152 64 0">
<keywords1> <item word="sim.getObject" autocomplete="true" calltip="int handle=sim.getObject(string path)" /> <item word="sim.getObjectPosition" autocomplete="true" calltip="vector3 pos=sim.getObjectPosition(int handle, int relHandle)" /> </keywords1>
<keywords2> <item word="sim.getObjectQuaternion" autocomplete="true" calltip="quaternion quat=sim.getObjectQuaternion(int handle, int relHandle)" /> </keywords2>
</editor>
placement attribute recognizes following values: center, absolute and relative

method.name: textEditorShow
method.description: Shows or hides a text edition window previously opened with sim.textEditorOpen

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of the text editor window

param.name: showState
param.description: desired show state of the text editor window

method.name: transformBuffer
method.description: Modifies a buffer that contains packed data

param.name: inBuffer
param.description: input buffer that contains packed data

param.name: outFormat
param.description: desired buffer type for the returned buffer

param.name: mult
param.description: multiplier value. We have out = offset + multiplier * in

param.name: offOrSplitSize
param.description: offset or split size value. We have out = offset + multiplier * in

param.name: outBuff
param.description: the modified buffer or the various split buffers

method.name: setEventFilters
method.description: Specifies filters for the sysCall_event callback. Operates on a per script basis

param.name: filters

method.name: removeFromCollection
method.description: Removes an item from a collection

param.name: collectionHandle
param.description: handle of a collection.

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of an object

param.name: what
param.description:
type of object (or group of objects) to remove. Following are allowed values:
sim.handle_single (for a single object)
sim.handle_all (for all objects in the scene)
sim.handle_tree (for a tree of objects)
sim.handle_chain (for a chain of objects (i.e. an inverted tree)).

param.name: options
param.description: sim.handle_single (for a single object)

method.name: auxiliaryConsolePrint
method.description: Prints to an auxiliary console window

param.name: consoleHandle
param.description: handle of the console window, previously returned by the sim.auxiliaryConsoleOpen

param.name: text
param.description: text to append, or an empty string to clear the console window

method.name: broadcastMsg
method.description: Broadcasts a message to all scripts, except for the emitting script. Messages are received synchronously via the sysCall_msg callback function

param.name: message
param.description: message to broadcast. Best is to use following skeleton message: message = {'id': 'msgIdentifyingString', 'data': [...]}

param.name: options
param.description: not used, keep at 0

method.name: launchExecutable
method.description: Launches an executable. Similar to os.execute or io.popen, but is system independent

param.name: filename
param.description: file name of the executable. If the filename starts with '@', then it will be considered as a system command, otherwise the current directory might be automatically prepended to the filename if it makes sense.

param.name: parameters
param.description: optional input arguments

param.name: showStatus
param.description: 0 to hide the application's window, 1 to show it. Works only with Windows OS.

method.name: pushUserEvent
method.description: Pushes a user-triggered event. Messages are received asynchronously via the sysCall_event callback function and via the plugin sim_message_eventcallback_events message call

param.name: event
param.description: event name

param.name: handle
param.description: optional handle associated with the event. Can be -1

param.name: eventData
param.description: optional unique id associated with the event. Can be -1

param.name: options
param.description: event data

method.name: setGraphStreamTransformation
method.description: Applies a transformation to a graph stream

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph

param.name: streamId
param.description: id of the stream

param.name: mult
param.description: multiplication factor

param.name: off
param.description: an offset

param.name: movingAvgPeriod
param.description: moving average period. Set to 1 for no moving average

method.name: setJointTargetForce
method.description: Sets the force or torque that a joint can exert

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: forceOrTorque
param.description: maximum force or torque the joint can exert

param.name: signedValue
param.description: if true, the sign of the force/torque indicates the desired movement direction

method.name: setJointTargetPosition
method.description: Sets the target linear/angular position of a joint. When in kinematic mode, the joint moves according to a motion profile that respects maximum velocity, acceleration and jerk values. In dynamic and position/custom control mode, the controller is instructed about the desired position

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: targetPosition
param.description: target position of the joint

param.name: motionParams
param.description: when in kinematic mode: the maximum allowed velocity, acceleration and jerk. When in dynamics mode with position control and motion profile enabled, the maximum allowed velocity, acceleration and jerk. Can be None/nil for default values. See also the maxVelAccelJerk property

method.name: setJointTargetVelocity
method.description: Sets the target linear/angular velocity of a non-spherical joint. When in kinematic mode, the joint moves according to a motion profile that respects maximum acceleration and jerk values. In dynamic and velocity control mode, the controller is instructed about the desired velocity

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: targetVelocity
param.description: target velocity of the joint

param.name: motionParams
param.description: when the joint is in kinematic mode: the maximum allowed acceleration and jerk. When in dynamic mode with motion profile control enabled: the maximum allowed acceleration and jerk. Can be None/nil for default values. See also the maxVelAccelJerk property

method.name: setShapeTexture
method.description: Applies/removes a texture to/from a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape.

param.name: textureId
param.description: ID of the texture or -1 to remove any existing texture. See also sim.getTextureId , sim.getShapeTextureId and sim.createTexture

param.name: mappingMode
param.description: texture mapping mode .

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0: if set (1), then adjacent texture pixels are not interpolated.
bit1: if set (2), then the texture is applied as a decal (its appearance won't be influenced by light conditions).
bit2: if set (4), then the texture will be repeated along the U direction.
bit3: if set (8), then the texture will be repeated along the V direction.

param.name: uvScaling
param.description: bit0: if set (1), then adjacent texture pixels are not interpolated.

param.name: position
param.description: bit1: if set (2), then the texture is applied as a decal (its appearance won't be influenced by light conditions).

param.name: orientation
param.description: bit2: if set (4), then the texture will be repeated along the U direction.

method.name: writeTexture
method.description: Overwrites a specific texture (or a portion of it) with RGB data

param.name: textureId
param.description: ID of the texture. See also sim.getTextureId

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 reserved. Do not set
bit1 reserved. Do not set
bit2 set (4)=only an elliptical/circular portion of the texture data will be written

param.name: textureData
param.description: reserved. Do not set

param.name: posX
param.description: reserved. Do not set

param.name: posY
param.description: set (4)=only an elliptical/circular portion of the texture data will be written

param.name: sizeX
param.description: RGB data to write onto the texture. Each pixel is represented with 3 bytes (0-255)

param.name: sizeY
param.description: x/y position where to copy the RGB data. Set to 0/0 to overwrite the full texture

param.name: interpolation
param.description: x/y size of the RGB data. Set to 0/0 to overwrite the full texture
